_doc: The reward-cost calibration knobs (θ/λ/H/κ/weights/priors). Structure is designed; VALUES are tuned per deployment. A .grgn contract's formula.reward_cost block overrides any of these per agent (contract.reward_cost_cfg → cfg_with). Meanings: theta = worth-it threshold on CE (act iff CE > θ); lambda = risk aversion (CE = μ − (λ/2)σ²); H = holding/WIP cost per open step; kappa = irreversibility cost in a leaf's cost — the EPISTEMIC penalty for forfeiting act-observe-correct (≈ one entity-unit, so a destructive irreversible act never prices below the reversible allocation it undoes; destructiveness itself stays in the consent gate); w_resource/w_time = weights on resource commitment / time; time = default per-leaf time estimate; p_world = default per-leaf world-success probability; R = default reward for closing the ROOT goal; beta/gamma = how hard p_self raises θ / λ; rho_min = min acceptable branch success prob (sets the depth budget); alpha = sub-goal reward share (0 = book only at root); p_world_k = Beta-prior pseudocount for LEARNING p_world.
